task:
Build a TodoList CLI in Python using typer + SQLite.

IMPORTANT: Use the following FEATURES.md EXACTLY — do NOT implement more than 1-2 features per loop. The mutation caps are strict.

## F-001: Project scaffold
Create src/todolist/__init__.py (empty), src/todolist/db.py with get_connection() and init_db() (todos table: id INTEGER PK, title TEXT NOT NULL, done INTEGER DEFAULT 0, priority INTEGER DEFAULT 0, due_date TEXT).
tests/test_db.py: 3 tests (connection ok, init_db creates table, idempotent).

## F-002: CLI entry + add command
src/todolist/main.py: typer app, app() entry.
"add" command: todolist add <title> [--priority 0-2] → inserts row, prints "Added: <title> (id=N)".
tests/test_add.py: add succeeds, duplicate titles ok, empty title raises error.

## F-003: list command
"list" command: todolist list → prints all todos (id, title, priority, done, due_date). Empty list prints "No todos.".
tests/test_list.py: empty list, list with items, shows correct fields.

## F-004: complete command
"complete" command: todolist complete <id> → sets done=1. Prints "Done: <title>". Error if id not found.
tests/test_complete.py: complete existing, complete missing id errors, already-done is idempotent.

## F-005: delete command
"delete" command: todolist delete <id> → removes row. Prints "Deleted: <title>". Error if id not found.
tests/test_delete.py: delete existing (verify gone), delete missing errors.

## F-006: search command
"search" command: todolist search <query> → case-insensitive search in title. Prints matching todos.
tests/test_search.py: finds match, no results, case-insensitive.

## F-007: priority filter
"list --priority N" flag → filter by priority level (0, 1, 2).
tests/test_priority.py: add items with different priorities, filter shows only correct level.

## F-008: due date
"add --due YYYY-MM-DD" flag → stores due_date. "list --overdue" → shows todos with due_date < today.
tests/test_due.py: add with due date, list overdue, list not overdue excluded.

## F-009: export
"export" command: todolist export [--file out.json] → writes todos to JSON. Defaults to stdout.
tests/test_export.py: export produces valid JSON, all fields present.

## F-010: import
"import" command: todolist import <file.json> → reads JSON, inserts todos (skips duplicates by title).
tests/test_import.py: import round-trips with export, duplicates skipped.

setup: python -m pip install typer pytest ruff mypy pip-audit

printf '[project]
name = "todolist"
version = "0.1.0"
requires-python = ">=3.11"

[tool.ruff]
line-length = 88
select = ["E", "F", "W", "I"]

[tool.mypy]
strict = true
warn_return_any = true
warn_unused_configs = true

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests"]
pythonpath = ["src"]
' > pyproject.toml

program_md:
# PROGRAM.md — Autoresearch Program Definition

## Product: TodoList CLI

## Objective

Maximize composite quality score through hypothesis-driven mutations.
Each loop implements EXACTLY ONE feature from FEATURES.md — no more.

## Mutation Scope

- Max files changed per loop: 2
- Max files created per loop: 1
- Max diff lines per loop: 80
- Max endpoint/route changes per loop: 0

## Eval Protocol

weights:
  tests: 0.45
  lint: 0.25
  typecheck: 0.20
  coverage: 0.05
  security: 0.05

## Keep/Discard Policy

- keep_threshold: score_after > score_before
- tie_policy: discard

## Budget

- max_loops: 15
- max_wall_seconds: 32400
- max_discards_in_a_row: 3

## Stop Conditions

- target_score: 1.00
- min_improvement_delta: 0.01
- max_plateau_loops: 2
- consecutive_discards >= max_discards_in_a_row

## Hypothesis Sources

- FEATURES.md (implement exactly one feature per loop), eval failures

## Arena Rules

- Each hypothesis MUST target exactly ONE feature from FEATURES.md
- DO NOT implement multiple features in a single loop — caps will reject it
- One feature = one test file + one source change = at most 80 diff lines
- If tests break, fix them in the SAME loop before committing
